title: config-validate: loud refusal of malformed / prompt-less / unsafe cli tiers (W-e/AO12, B2, B6/B7)
action: Add a `bee config validate` verb + NEW shared validator in state.mjs (do NOT touch resolveTier — it is the fail-open hook hot path via guard:133->modelForTier->resolveTier; a throw bricks the guard). Register mirrored (both template + .bee/bin copies): registry entry config.validate in command-registry.mjs, handleConfigValidate in bee.mjs, its HANDLERS row. Validator inspects .bee/config.json models.<runtime>.<slot> and REFUSES loudly (named, non-zero) where normalizeTierValue->undefined + normalizeModels:228 today silently revert to seeded sonnet (state.mjs:142): (a) cli value missing kind:'cli' or non-empty command [W-e]; (b) cli command that cannot receive a prompt — config must DECLARE prompt transport explicitly [B2]; (c) cli command carrying an unsafe flag. SECURITY (panel): this is a BLOCKLIST OF KNOWN-BAD, not a read-only guarantee — enumerate alias closure: --yolo, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox, --full-auto, -s danger-full-access (+ agy equiv). Also surface as a `bee status --json` warning via handleStatus (bee.mjs:529). Cite AO12, W-e, B2, B6, B7.

truths: NEW validator function; resolveTier unchanged (no throw on hook path).
Malformed/prompt-less/unsafe cli tier -> NAMED refusal, never silent sonnet revert.
Unsafe-flag check is a documented known-bad BLOCKLIST, NOT proof of sandboxing; env/wrapper/alias injection is explicitly out of reach and handed to a later slice, not claimed resolved.
Runs in `bee config validate` AND `bee status --json` warning (AO12).
artifacts: scripts/test_config_validate.mjs: valid model/cli tier, malformed->refuse, prompt-less->refuse, each unsafe alias->refuse, + null/wrong-type malformed-input row.
Verb reachable via `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs config validate` (both copies).
prohibitions: No resolveTier change / no throw on hook path.
Do NOT claim B6 capability gap (cli gather has no write-guard) resolved — only the config-declaration half.
No AO5 model-equality (2A-iii), no cli gather branch (2A-ii).

trace.outcome: validateModelsConfig + UNSAFE_CLI_FLAGS blocklist (both mirrors); bee config validate verb; status --json joins validator warnings; required promptVia transport (B2); 24-case test. Silent-revert-to-sonnet now a loud named refusal.
trace.verification_evidence:
First-hand verify by orchestrator (not worker assertion):

$ node scripts/test_config_validate.mjs
SUMMARY: 24/24 passed  (exit 0)
  covers: valid model tier; valid cli tier (declared promptVia, safe); cli-malformed (missing command); cli-prompt-transport-missing; each unsafe alias (--yolo, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox, --full-auto, -s danger-full-access) individually + multi-alias; null/undefined/wrong-type inputs never throw.

$ node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs
PASS templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (19 files)
PASS runtime-derived hook inventory byte-identical (9 files)  (exit 0)

$ node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs
334 passed, 0 failed  (exit 0)

$ node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs
141 passed, 0 failed  (exit 0)

$ node .bee/bin/bee.mjs config validate --json
{ "ok": false, "problem_count": 2, "problems": [
  { "code": "cli-prompt-transport-missing", "runtime": "claude", "slot": "advisor", ... },
  { "code": "cli-prompt-transport-missing", "runtime": "codex", "slot": "review", ... } ] }
-> the new verb runs end-to-end and correctly flags the repo's own two cli tier values (previously silent). Behavior change proven: a config shape that used to silently revert to sonnet now produces a named, non-zero refusal + a status warning.
trace.verify_output: SUMMARY: 24/24 passed (exit 0). Regressions green: test_lib_mirror 4/4 (19 lib + 9 hook byte-identical), test_lib 334/334, test_bee_cli 141/141. New verb reachable: 'bee config validate --json' correctly flags repo's own models.claude.advisor + models.codex.review as cli-prompt-transport-missing.
